purpose: Prospectively expand the MAPK14 receptor pool using label-independent structural evidence, add a matched linear top-k baseline, and require train-only QUBO non-degeneracy before any new validation evaluation.

upstream_failed_gate.observed_failure: coverage-QUBO selected target_size=1 and was identical to single-best

data_boundary.development_train: available for future expanded-receptor docking and train-only method selection
data_boundary.previous_development_validation: consumed_diagnostic_only; prohibited for tuning, promotion, or post-hoc receptor selection
data_boundary.new_validation: not yet sampled, prepared, docked, or authorized

rcsb_discovery.source: RCSB PDB Search API and Data API

coordinate_eligibility.alignment: proper-rotation Kabsch alignment using sequence-matched protein C-alpha atoms
coordinate_eligibility.alternate_location_rule: highest occupancy, then blank, A, and lexical tie-break
structural_features: pairwise pocket side-chain heavy-atom centroid distances

pool_expansion.selection_rule: deterministic max-min structural-distance selection seeded by the four existing receptors
pool_expansion.failure_action: stop without ligand docking if fewer than four new candidates pass coordinate eligibility

future_train_only_method_gate.score_normalization: fit per-receptor min-max bounds on each inner-training fold only

train_only_non_degeneracy_gate.failure_action: stop before sampling or docking a new validation panel

future_validation.authorization_condition: a separate preregistration may be created only after structural eligibility, redocking, expanded train docking, and the train-only non-degeneracy gate all pass

interpretation_boundary: Structural expansion can create a meaningful combinatorial search space, but it cannot itself establish screening complementarity or QUBO benefit. A QUBO-specific claim additionally requires improvement over matched linear top-k, classical, random, and all-receptor baselines on untouched data.
